channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US refuses to blame Russia for Ukraine war, splitting with European allies in UN votes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In a dramatic shift in transatlantic relations under President Donald Trump, the United States split with its European allies by refusing to blame Russia for its invasion of Ukraine in votes on three U.N. resolutions Monday seeking an end to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Western leaders visit Kyiv and pledge military support against Russia on the war's 3rd anniversary
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine on Monday marked the bleakest anniversary yet of its war against the Russia invasion, with the country's forces under severe pressure on the battlefield and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration apparently embracing the Kremlin in a reversal of American policy.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Macron project unity despite clear Ukraine-Russia divide
item.description: President Trump and French President Emmanuel Macron sought to project unity during a White House meeting Monday, even as cracks opened further in the alliance between the U.S. and Europe over the path forward in Ukraine. The two met at the Oval Office as the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: Ukraine marks third anniversary of war as US shifts its policy under Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A dozen leaders from Europe and Canada are in Ukraine to mark the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion and discuss support with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. They include European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Xi says China, Russia true friends as Trump pursues Putin talks
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping says China and Russia are true friends after speaking on the phone with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: A police union in Haiti demands more protection a day after gangs kill a Kenyan policeman
item.description: Haitian police officers are demanding better protection and treatment a day after gunmen killed a Kenyan policeman who was part of a U.N.-backed mission tasked with fighting gangs in the troubled Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Evens Sanon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: German election winner vows 'real independence' from US
item.description: Friedrich Merz, the leader of Germany's center-right Christian Democrats, who finished first in Sunday's election, has vowed “real independence from the USA" as he sets about forming a coalition government, Reuters reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: More pressure on Rwanda as Congo says rebel uprising has killed over 7,000 people this year
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — More than 7,000 people have died this year as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels have captured unprecedented amounts of territory in mineral-rich eastern Congo, Congo's prime minister said Monday, as the European Union announced it would review an agreement with Rwanda on critical raw m
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and WILSON MCMAKIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Germany’s new conservative leader looks to ‘achieve independence’ from US
item.description: Germany's incoming chancellor, Friedrich Merz, has said an "absolute priority" for him will be to distance Europe from the U.S. following comments by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A witchcraft case involving Zambia's president brings scrutiny of a colonial-era law and traditions
item.description: LUSAKA, Zambia (AP) — Two men are on trial in Zambia on charges of practicing witchcraft and possessing charms intended to harm the country's president, in a case that has become a source of fascination in the southern African nation.
item.dc:creator: JACOB ZIMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On third anniversary of Ukraine invasion, European leaders show support, express unease
item.description: European leaders voiced support for Ukraine on the third anniversary of the Russia invasion while demanding a say in any US-Russia peace arrangement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The biggest takeaways from Germany's election, which will bring change to the EU's leading power
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany faces its second change of leader in fewer than four years after the head of the center-right opposition, Friedrich Merz, won Sunday's election, which saw a surge for a far-right party and a stinging defeat for outgoing Chancellor Olaf Scholz.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis remains in critical condition but has shown slight improvement in laboratory tests.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's hopeful Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conservative opposition wins German election and the far right is 2nd with strongest postwar result
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The opposition conservatives led by Friedrich Merz won a lackluster victory in Germany's election Sunday and Alternative for Germany doubled its support in the strongest showing for a far-right party since World War II, projections showed.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sudan’s military breaks paramilitary group's siege of crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than yearlong siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservatives poised to lead Germany; far-right AfD second
item.description: German conservative opposition leader Friedrich Merz appears poised to win the country’s election Sunday, while the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party places second despite making gains.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israel moves tanks into West Bank for first time since 2002
item.description: Israel sent tanks into the West Bank on Sunday for the first time since 2002 and told its military to prepare for "an extended stay" to "thwart terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis remains in 'critical condition' with signs of early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis remains hospitalized in "critical condition" with pneumonia, but the Vatican says he rested well overnight.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis shares written message while continuing hospitalization
item.description: Pope Francis purportedly shared a written message from his hospital bed while remaining in critical condition and continuing treatment in Rome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis' health 'looking very serious': Vatican reporter
item.description: When journalist Colm Flynn last saw Pope Francis, the Holy Father was “in good spirits” but visibly “struggling to catch his breath.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Isolated in 'harsh conditions:' Deportee from US details legal limbo in Panama camp near Darien Gap
item.description: CARTI, Panama (AP) — A deportee from the United States detained in a camp in rural Panama, among a hundred who refused to return to their countries, described on Saturday waiting in limbo under “harsh conditions” and cut off from access to legal council and other rights.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in Hamas captivity
item.description: Edan Alexander spent most of his life in Tenafly, New Jersey, before moving to Israel and enlisting in the IDF. On Oct. 7, 2023, he was taken hostage and is still being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews: Investigators
item.description: The suspect arrived in Germany in 2023 as an unaccompanied minor and successfully applied for asylum, investigators said. He lives in Leipzig.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Economy, immigration, Elon Musk at center of German election; conservative candidate favored to win
item.description: Germans go to the polls Sunday. Chancellor Scholz's Social Democrats are likely to lose to the conservative CDU party, as the right-wing Alternative for Germany party is likely to make gains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu decries release of wrong body as a ceasefire violation. Hamas pledges to investigate
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vowed revenge Friday for what he described as a “cruel and malicious violation” of the ceasefire agreement after authorities determined that a body released by Hamas was not an Israeli mother of two small boys, as the militant group had prom
item.dc:creator: AREEJ HAZBOUN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 20 Mexican cities, 5 in US among 50 most dangerous in the world
item.description: At the top of the list is Haiti's Port-au-Prince with 139 homicides per 100,000 residents, followed by five cities in Mexico: Colima, Manzanillo, Acapulco, Tijuana and Ciudad Obregón.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing user data with TikTok owner ByteDance
item.description: "We confirmed DeepSeek communicating with ByteDance," a South Korean data protection regulator told Yonhap News Agency.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis has bilateral pneumonia but remains in good spirits, the Vatican says
item.description: Nevertheless the pope is in good spirits and is grateful for the prayers for his recovery, Vatican spokesman Matteo Bruni said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: Passengers live after escaping ‘upside down’ jet
item.description: Air traffic control audio captured the moment officials realized Delta Flight 4819 was burning on the tarmac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation
